description: Election model has createdAt DateTime field with @default(now())
steps: Read prisma/schema.prisma
Verify Election model has 'createdAt DateTime @default(now())'
Run npx prisma generate without errors

description: Election model has updatedAt DateTime field with @updatedAt
steps: Read prisma/schema.prisma
Verify Election model has 'updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt'
Run npx prisma generate without errors

description: Election model has @@unique([district, electionDate]) constraint
steps: Read prisma/schema.prisma
Verify Election model has '@@unique([district, electionDate])'
Attempt to insert two Elections with same district+electionDate
Verify second insert fails with unique constraint error

description: Candidate model has @@unique([electionId, name]) constraint
steps: Read prisma/schema.prisma
Verify Candidate model has '@@unique([electionId, name])'
Attempt to insert two Candidates with same electionId+name
Verify second insert fails with unique constraint error

description: Prisma migration for Civic API support runs successfully
steps: Run npx prisma db push
Verify migration completes without errors
Verify new columns and constraints exist in database

description: src/lib/civic-api.ts exists with CivicContest and CivicElectionResult interfaces
steps: Verify file src/lib/civic-api.ts exists
Verify CivicContest interface has office, level, district, candidates fields
Verify CivicElectionResult interface has election, contests, normalizedInput fields
Verify TypeScript compiles without errors

description: lookupElections calls correct Google Civic API endpoint with address and key params
steps: Verify lookupElections is exported from civic-api.ts
Verify it calls https://www.googleapis.com/civicinfo/v2/voterInfoQuery
Verify address parameter is passed to the API
Verify key parameter uses GOOGLE_CIVIC_API_KEY env var

description: lookupElections passes returnAllAvailableData=true to API
steps: Read src/lib/civic-api.ts
Verify returnAllAvailableData=true is included in API request params
This is critical — without it, bare zip codes cause API errors

description: lookupElections has 15-second timeout via AbortSignal.timeout()
steps: Read src/lib/civic-api.ts
Verify fetch call includes signal: AbortSignal.timeout(15_000)
Verify timeout value is 15 seconds

description: lookupElections throws on HTTP error responses
steps: Read src/lib/civic-api.ts
Verify function checks response.ok or response.status
Verify it throws an Error on non-2xx responses
Unit test with mocked 500 response confirms throw

description: lookupElections returns empty contests array (not throw) when no election data
steps: Read src/lib/civic-api.ts
Verify function handles API response with empty or missing contests
Verify it returns object with contests: [] instead of throwing
Unit test with mock empty-contests response confirms empty array

description: Elections route removes Census Geocoder imports and ZIP_TO_DISTRICT constant
steps: Read src/app/api/elections/route.ts
Verify no import from @/lib/geocoding
Verify no ZIP_TO_DISTRICT constant defined
Verify no reference to Census Geocoder or geocodeAddress

description: Address param triggers Civic API lookup and returns upserted elections
steps: Send GET /api/elections?address=123+Main+St+Madison+WI
Verify response is JSON array of Election objects
Verify elections were upserted into database
Verify response includes election name, district, type, electionDate, state

description: Zip param triggers Civic API lookup (zip passed as address string)
steps: Send GET /api/elections?zip=53703
Verify zip is passed to lookupElections as the address string
Verify response is JSON array of Election objects
Verify same Civic API codepath is used for both zip and address

description: Each Civic API contest is upserted as an Election row
steps: Call GET /api/elections?address=... with a valid address
Verify prisma.election.upsert is called for each contest
Verify upsert where-clause uses { district, electionDate }
Verify election data is persisted in database

description: Each candidate in each contest is upserted as a Candidate row
steps: Call GET /api/elections?address=... with a valid address
Verify prisma.candidate.upsert is called for each candidate in each contest
Verify upsert where-clause uses { electionId_name: { electionId, name } }
Verify candidate name, party, and officialWebsite are stored

description: Election name format: '{election.name} — {contest.office}'
steps: Read src/app/api/elections/route.ts
Verify election name uses em-dash separator between election name and contest office
Example output: '2026 Wisconsin General Election — State Assembly District 42'

description: Election.district is set from contest.district.name
steps: Read src/app/api/elections/route.ts
Verify district field is extracted from contest.district.name
Example: "Wisconsin's 42nd State Assembly district"

description: Scope-to-type mapping correctly maps all 14 district.scope values to Election.type
steps: Read the scope mapping code in elections route or civic-api module
Verify national → federal
Verify congressional → federal
Verify statewide, stateUpper, stateLower → state
Verify countywide, countyCouncil, citywide, cityCouncil, ward, township, schoolBoard, judicial, special → local
Verify unknown/missing scope defaults to local

description: Election.state is set from normalizedInput.state (2-letter abbreviation)
steps: Read src/app/api/elections/route.ts
Verify state is extracted from Civic API response normalizedInput.state
Verify it stores 2-letter state abbreviation (e.g., 'WI')

description: Off-season: returns existing DB elections when Civic API returns empty contests
steps: Simulate Civic API returning zero contests (off-season scenario)
Verify route queries existing DB elections for the resolved state
Verify previously cached and seed data is returned to client
Verify no error is thrown

description: No-filter request (no zip/address) returns all elections unchanged
steps: Send GET /api/elections with no query params
Verify response contains all elections in database
Verify this behavior is identical to before Civic API integration

description: Civic API failure returns 422 error to client with meaningful message
steps: Simulate Civic API HTTP error or network timeout
Verify route returns HTTP 422 status
Verify response body has { error: '...' } with descriptive message
Verify no unhandled exception crashes the server

description: .env.example contains GOOGLE_CIVIC_API_KEY entry with instructions
steps: Read .env.example
Verify GOOGLE_CIVIC_API_KEY line exists with placeholder value
Verify comment explains: Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Enable Civic API → Create key

description: geocoding.ts has @deprecated JSDoc comment mentioning Civic API replacement
steps: Read src/lib/geocoding.ts
Verify @deprecated JSDoc tag is present on module or exported functions
Verify deprecation message mentions Google Civic Information API as replacement

description: geocoding.ts still exists (not deleted) to preserve test imports
steps: Verify src/lib/geocoding.ts file exists on disk
Verify its function bodies are intact
Verify npm run test still passes (no broken imports)

description: Election upsert on (district, electionDate) is idempotent — no duplicates
steps: Call GET /api/elections?address=... twice with same address
Verify same Election rows returned both times (same IDs)
Query database directly: no duplicate (district, electionDate) pairs
Verify updatedAt timestamp is refreshed on second call

description: Candidate upsert on (electionId, name) is idempotent — no duplicates
steps: Call GET /api/elections?address=... twice with same address
Verify same Candidate rows exist after both calls (same IDs)
Query database directly: no duplicate (electionId, name) pairs

description: quiz/page.tsx works without code changes — Election[] response shape unchanged
steps: Navigate to /quiz in browser
Enter a zip code and submit
Verify elections list is displayed correctly
Verify election selection shows candidates
Verify Start Quiz button navigates to quiz page

description: Election.district shows full descriptive names from Civic API in the UI
steps: Call GET /api/elections?zip=53703
Verify district field shows full name like "Wisconsin's 42nd State Assembly district"
Verify this renders correctly in the election list on /quiz page

description: Unit tests for civic-api.ts cover various contest types (federal, state, local)
steps: Run npm run test
Verify test file exists for civic-api module
Verify tests mock fetch with federal, state, and local contest responses
Verify parsed output matches expected CivicElectionResult structure

description: Unit tests for civic-api.ts cover empty results and API errors
steps: Run npm run test
Verify test for empty contests → returns { contests: [] }
Verify test for HTTP 500 → throws Error
Verify test for network timeout → throws Error

description: npm run build passes with zero TypeScript and lint errors after all changes
steps: Run npm run build
Verify exit code 0
Run npm run lint
Verify exit code 0

description: All existing tests (24+) continue to pass — no regressions
steps: Run npm run test
Verify all pre-existing tests pass
Verify total test count is >= 24 (existing) + new civic-api tests
